verdict_basis: Three reviewers (architecture, quality, reality) returned GO-WITH-CHANGES; systems returned NO-GO. The convergent picture upgrades systems' NO-GO call rather than overriding it: every blocker systems flagged is independently corroborated by 2-3 other lenses, and the highest-convergence finding (vocabulary drift) was flagged by FIVE reviewers across the two plans. The structural design — leaf-component decomposition, centralized metadata table, unknown-flag fallback, PluginSummary type extension — is sound. The defects are vocabulary fidelity, a TypeScript compile break left for a sibling plan to clean up, and a regression-coverage drop hidden inside a full-file replacement. All are surgical fixes to plan text; no architectural rework needed.
summary: Four blockers must be fixed before 16b is executed: (B1) vocabulary drift — 'emits_provenance' is not in the backend's 13-member VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS (correct name is 'provenance'), 'retention' and 'network' are missing entirely from the AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS table; (B2) no parity test gates KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS against the backend allowlist — the drift that produced B1 will recur on every backend addition; (B3) PluginCard's onCloseDrawer prop deletion leaves a TypeScript compile error at CatalogDrawer.tsx:359 with no tsc gate in Task 5; (B4) Task 5's full-file replacement of PluginCard.test.tsx silently deletes 11 regression tests (8 discriminated-union + 3 error/loading-state) that pin production code paths surviving unchanged. Three should-fix items remain (dead CSS, aria-label query drift on retained tests, retain ChatInput comment update). The structural approach is approved.

convergence_findings.title: Closed-vocabulary drift between 16a backend allowlist and 16b frontend metadata table

sources.quality: F1+F2 (same three drifts) + F3 (no parity test)
sources.systems: SYS-001 (cause + impact) + SYS-003 (structural cause: no gate)

convergence_findings.evidence: Backend VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS (16a:1181-1197) has 13 members: io_read, io_write, external_call, deterministic, seeded, non_deterministic, provenance, retention, quarantine, coerce, signed, network, credentials. 16b AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS table (16b:482-590) has 11 entries with one wrong key: 'emits_provenance' (16b:563) where 16a uses 'provenance'; 'retention' and 'network' have NO entries. 16a's round-2 review (16a:2323) explicitly recorded the 'emits_provenance' → 'provenance' rename — never propagated to 16b. The forward-compatibility unknown-flag fallback specifically designed for future additions instead masks present misalignment for 23% of the current vocabulary. The canonical demo CSV source which 16a Task 5 annotates with audit_characteristics={'provenance', 'quarantine'} will show 'provenance' as a grey 'unknown' chip on demo day.
convergence_findings.resolution: In 16b Task 2 Step 3 auditCharacteristics.ts: (1) Replace flag 'emits_provenance' with 'provenance' (label 'extra provenance' may stay). (2) Add metadata entries for 'retention' (label 'retention policy', positive tone) and 'network' (label 'network', attention tone — distinct from 'external_call' as 16a treats them as distinct author-declarable flags). (3) In Task 2 Step 1 auditCharacteristics.test.ts, update lookupAuditCharacteristic('emits_provenance') → lookupAuditCharacteristic('provenance') and add assertions for retention and network. (4) Add the parity test described in C2-PARITY-GATE.

convergence_findings.title: No mechanical parity gate between backend VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS and frontend KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS
convergence_findings.evidence: Backend has test_all_plugin_audit_characteristics_are_valid (16a:1278). Frontend has no equivalent gate. Without one, the drift in C1-VOCAB-DRIFT will recur on every backend vocabulary addition: a 14th characteristic added to 16a renders as a grey unknown chip with no test failure. The 'forward-compatibility null fallback' design philosophy is correct for future-true unknowns but actively masks present alignment defects.
convergence_findings.confidence: HIGH (2 reviewers; structurally load-bearing — without this gate, C1 keeps recurring)
convergence_findings.resolution: Add to auditCharacteristics.test.ts a parity test: declare a local BACKEND_VOCAB constant matching the 13-member VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS, then `for (const f of BACKEND_VOCAB) { expect(KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS).toContain(f); }`. Frontend-only, no Python import. Cheap, fail-loud on the next addition. This is the structural fix that prevents C1 from being a single point of attention forever.

convergence_findings.title: onCloseDrawer prop removal breaks CatalogDrawer.tsx:359 with no tsc gate in Task 5

sources.quality: F5 (MAJOR; ranks lower because quality treats it as recoverable via add-tsc-step)

convergence_findings.evidence: CatalogDrawer.tsx:359 passes onCloseDrawer={onClose} to PluginCard. Task 5 deletes onCloseDrawer from PluginCardProps. 16b's modified-files list (16b:129-156) does NOT include CatalogDrawer.tsx. Task 5 Step 5 runs vitest only, NOT tsc --noEmit. After Task 5 commits, `npx tsc --noEmit` fails with a TypeScript excess-property error on the CatalogDrawer call site. Task 1 Step 5 runs tsc but BEFORE Task 5 deletes the prop. The plan states 'Expected: PASS' at Task 5 Step 5 — this is wrong. CLAUDE.md No-Legacy: 'Change all call sites in the same commit.'
convergence_findings.resolution: Add CatalogDrawer.tsx to Task 5's modified-files list. In Task 5 Step 3, remove the onCloseDrawer={onClose} prop from CatalogDrawer.tsx:359 in the same commit. Add `npx tsc --noEmit` to Task 5 Step 5 after the vitest regression sweep, before the commit. (Alternative — retain onCloseDrawer as an unused optional prop until 16c — REJECTED because the No-Legacy policy disfavours leaving dead prop interfaces; the in-same-commit cleanup is the canonical fix.)

convergence_findings.title: Task 5 full-file replacement of PluginCard.test.tsx silently deletes 11 regression guards on unchanged production paths

sources.reality: F7 (NIT/coverage; reality lens does not score severity here but documents the same gap)

convergence_findings.evidence: Existing PluginCard.test.tsx:1-9 documents itself as a regression guard for bug elspeth-dcf12c061b. Tests at :115-292 (8 discriminated-union tests covering one-section-per-variant, required-badge scoping, fallback-to-def-name, variant-prefix-default, non-local-$ref skipping, dangling-$ref drop, no-properties variant) and :294-341 (3 error/loading tests: schemaError banner + Retry, aria-live spinner, onExpand-exactly-once-per-expand) pin code paths that the rewritten PluginCard.tsx (16b:1093-1244) retains verbatim. The plan's replacement covers 10 tests of which none replicate the discriminated-union, dangling-$ref, or error-state paths. Production code survives; its guard does not. F7 cascade: existing tests use getByRole('button', { name: '${name} plugin details' }); rewrite changes aria-label to 'Schema for ${name}' — retained tests need parallel query update.
convergence_findings.resolution: Change Task 5 Step 1 to PRESERVE the discriminated-union describe block, the flat-schema describe block, and the error/loading-state describe block. Remove ONLY the `describe('PluginCard — Use in pipeline action')` block (4 tests) and the nested-interactive structural-invariant test. Rewrite the collapsed-header describe to cover the new layout. Update all retained getByRole queries from '${name} plugin details' to /schema/i or 'Schema for ${name}'. The plan's 10 new tests can coexist with the retained 11 tests.

blocking_issues.title: Vocabulary drift: emits_provenance / missing retention / missing network
blocking_issues.resolution: See C1-VOCAB-DRIFT. Same-commit edit to auditCharacteristics.ts + auditCharacteristics.test.ts.

blocking_issues.title: Missing vocabulary parity test (no CI gate between backend and frontend vocabularies)
blocking_issues.resolution: See C2-PARITY-GATE. One Vitest test in auditCharacteristics.test.ts. Structural — without this, B1 recurs on every backend vocabulary addition. Higher priority than B1 because it gates B1's recurrence.

blocking_issues.title: onCloseDrawer prop deletion breaks CatalogDrawer.tsx with no tsc gate
blocking_issues.resolution: See C3-ONCLOSEDRAWER-BREAK. Add CatalogDrawer.tsx to Task 5 modified files; remove prop at line 359 in same commit; add tsc gate to Task 5 Step 5.

blocking_issues.title: Task 5 full-file replacement deletes 11 regression tests on unchanged production code paths
blocking_issues.resolution: See C4-REGRESSION-COVERAGE-DROP. Preserve discriminated-union + flat-schema + error/loading describe blocks; rewrite only the collapsed-header and use-in-pipeline blocks; update retained aria-label queries to match the new 'Schema for {name}' pattern.

warnings.title: Dead CSS (`.plugin-card-use-btn` and hover/focus variants) violates No-Legacy Policy
warnings.evidence: App.css:3373-3389 — four rule blocks for .plugin-card-use-btn that becomes dead code after Task 5 button removal. 16b's 'Out of scope: CSS' note conflates NEW class names (legitimately deferred) with DEAD CSS (must be deleted now per CLAUDE.md No-Legacy).
warnings.resolution: Delete the .plugin-card-use-btn rule blocks from App.css in the Task 5 commit. New class names (.plugin-card-audit-strip, .trust-tier-badge-*, etc.) remain legitimately deferred to the CSS pass.

warnings.title: title-attribute tooltips are not reliably accessible on touch/all screen readers
warnings.evidence: AuditCharacteristicIcon and TrustTierBadge use HTML title attribute for tooltips. Plan notes 'without pulling in a tooltip library'. NVDA/VoiceOver require specific settings; touch devices provide no hover; the substantive tier explanations are hidden behind hover.
warnings.resolution: Informational for 16b — acceptable for the demo bar. Before Phase 8 polish, replace with aria-describedby + offscreen text or a lightweight tooltip primitive. Verify against existing codebase tooltip usage; do not add a library without checking imports.

warnings.title: ChatInput.tsx:63 comment becomes stale (load-bearing comment per CLAUDE.md)
warnings.evidence: ChatInput.tsx:63 reads '// Listen for prefill events dispatched by PluginCard Use in pipeline action.' After Task 5 lands, PluginCard no longer dispatches; the new dispatcher is InlineChatSourceEntry (in 16c). CLAUDE.md: 'Comments are your institutional memory. Treat comments as load-bearing.'
warnings.resolution: Add ChatInput.tsx to Task 5's modified files; update line 63 comment to: 'Dispatched by InlineChatSourceEntry (catalog drawer) when the synthetic source entry is clicked.' One-line edit.

warnings.title: TrustTierBadge bare 'Tier 1/2/3' labels risk Linda-persona misread without color/semantic framing
warnings.evidence: Tooltips correctly explain tiers but rely on hover (W2). Non-technical persona may assign natural-language meaning to ordinals — 'Tier 3' reads as 'most risky' (matches the ELSPETH tier-3 framing) but could read as 'most capable' depending on prior frame. CSS pass color choice carries the load.
warnings.resolution: In the CSS pass: do NOT use red/danger for tier 3 (would confirm the 'risky' misread but also weaponize a neutral semantic). Use neutral-but-distinct treatment. Consider replacing bare 'Tier 3' label with 'External boundary' for tier 3, 'Pipeline data' for tier 2, 'Audit data' for tier 1. OPERATOR-DECISION (see below).

warnings.title: io_write attention-tone flags every sink entry with a yellow chip
warnings.evidence: All sink plugins have io_write via determinism derivation. Yellow attention chip on every Sinks-tab entry communicates 'all sinks are risky' which is overstatement.
warnings.resolution: CSS pass decision: either (a) demote io_write to muted/informational tone, or (b) suppress io_write from the strip for sink-type plugins (it is the definitional characteristic of a sink — non-informational to flag). Operator decision; document choice in the CSS pass.

warnings.title: initialExpanded test-only prop leaks into production PluginCardProps
warnings.evidence: 16b:1085 introduces initialExpanded?: boolean with comment 'Test-only'. Test-only props on production interfaces accumulate as anti-pattern. Alternative (userEvent.click + waitFor) is acceptable.
warnings.resolution: Implementer choice during execution: either delete initialExpanded and test by click, or retain with `@internal For testing only.` JSDoc. Either is acceptable; document the decision in the commit.

warnings.title: KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS typed as `string[]` rather than `readonly string[]`
warnings.evidence: Codebase uses `as const` tuples for similar fixed-list exports (e.g., VALIDATION_CHECK_OUTCOME_CODE_VALUES). KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS should follow.
warnings.resolution: Change export to `readonly string[]` derived via `AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS.map(m => m.flag) as const` (or equivalent). No functional change.

warnings.title: Task 1 test imports `expect` implicitly via vitest globals
warnings.evidence: 16b plan line 184 import statement omits `expect` while line 220 uses it. Works today (vitest globals=true) but inconsistent with codebase convention and brittle to config change.
warnings.resolution: Add `expect` to the import statement on line 184.

warnings.title: Stale comment 'Re-exported here' for PREFILL_CHAT_INPUT_EVENT after 2026-05-15 correction
warnings.evidence: 16b:1058 still has 'Re-exported here for backwards compatibility' wording — the 2026-05-15 review note already corrected this conceptually but the proposed code snippet retains the stale phrasing.
warnings.resolution: Replace with: 'Event name dispatched by InlineChatSourceEntry and consumed by ChatInput.tsx. Defined here because PluginCard.tsx is its historical home. Do not rename or move.'

recommendations.suggestion: B3 resolution restores the 16b/16c scope boundary. The primitives-vs-integration split is otherwise architecturally clean and should be preserved.
PluginSummary TypeScript extension is shape-correct against the 16a Pydantic wire format. PREFILL_CHAT_INPUT_EVENT export retention is the right call. No changes needed beyond the blockers.

16b is the metadata-table owner; the parity gate (C2-PARITY-GATE) is the right gate at the consumer side. 16a already gates declared flags via test_all_plugin_audit_characteristics_are_valid (its membership-of-vocab test). 16c is the chip-render consumer but does NOT need its own duplicate parity gate — the 16b gate covers KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS which feeds 16c's FilterChipStrip. One gate at the table owner is sufficient.

operator_decisions.question: Tier 1/2/3 semantic framing in TrustTierBadge
operator_decisions.context: Bare ordinals risk a Linda-persona misread (W4/SYS-004). Three options: (a) keep 'Tier 1/2/3' and rely on color/tooltip only; (b) replace with descriptive labels 'Audit data' / 'Pipeline data' / 'External boundary'; (c) hybrid — small ordinal + descriptive subtitle. Existing 08-catalog-reshape.md mockups (not consulted here) may carry the operator's intent.
operator_decisions.recommendation: Option (b) — descriptive labels — removes the misread vector entirely without relying on hover or color. Reverse the ordinal direction concern (tier 3 = bad? best? most external?) by replacing with semantic names. If operator prefers keeping the ordinals (for consistency with CLAUDE.md tier numbering), option (c).

operator_decisions.question: io_write tone for sink-type plugins
operator_decisions.context: Attention tone on every sink entry is technically correct (replay re-applies side effects) but overstates the daily-use risk (W5/SYS-006).
operator_decisions.recommendation: Demote to informational tone in the CSS pass, OR suppress on sink-type plugins (since it's the definitional characteristic of a sink). Either way, document the decision so the demote/suppress isn't reverted later.

what_is_good.structural_approach: Component decomposition (AuditCharacteristicIcon + TrustTierBadge + centralized auditCharacteristics.ts) is architecturally clean. Centralizing the flag-to-metadata mapping prevents drift between card and filter strip; the unknown-flag fallback is the right forward-compatibility mechanism (it just needs the parity gate to catch present drift).
what_is_good.tdd_shape: All five tasks follow failing-test-first with explicit run commands. Quality reviewer confirms: 'TDD shape is sound'.
what_is_good.typescript_type_discipline: DataTrustTier = 1|2|3 literal union forces discriminator reads at render time; TIER_TOOLTIPS exhaustively maps all tiers; consistent with codebase finite-value taxonomies.
what_is_good.scope_split: 16b primitives vs 16c integration boundary is honest (modulo B3 cleanup). 16b can ship alone and produce a visible-but-incomplete intermediate state — acceptable.
what_is_good.wire_contract_integrity: PluginSummary TS extension matches 16a Pydantic wire format exactly: string[] not tuple[str,...], DataTrustTier ge=1/le=3 maps to literal union, string|null on prose fields.
what_is_good.no_legacy_compliance: Plan removes buildInsertionPrompt, handleUseInPipeline, onCloseDrawer, and the Use-in-pipeline JSX entirely (modulo B3 call-site cleanup). PREFILL_CHAT_INPUT_EVENT export correctly retained for ChatInput continuity.

reality.key_signal: Two file-line-verified blockers (vocabulary drift, missing CatalogDrawer cleanup). One major (incomplete vocabulary).

architecture.key_signal: Three vocabulary mismatches and one TypeScript build-break. Structural approach approved.

quality.key_signal: Same 3 vocab drifts + no parity test + regression coverage drop hidden in full-file replacement.

systems.key_signal: Vocabulary divergence + onCloseDrawer break + no parity gate (structural cause). NO-GO upgraded by convergent evidence.

next_step: Fix the four blockers (B1-B4) in-session BEFORE 16b execution begins, in this order: (1) Edit auditCharacteristics.ts and its test to fix vocabulary drift (B1) AND add the parity test (B2) in the same plan diff — they are co-located changes. (2) Add CatalogDrawer.tsx to Task 5 modified files; remove onCloseDrawer call site at line 359; add `npx tsc --noEmit` to Task 5 Step 5 (B3). (3) Rewrite Task 5 Step 1 to preserve discriminated-union/flat-schema/error-loading describe blocks; update retained tests' aria-label queries to /schema/i (B4). (4) Address warnings W1 (dead CSS, same Task 5 commit) and W3 (ChatInput comment, add to Task 5 modified files). (5) Surface OD-1 and OD-2 to operator before staging smoke. Re-run reality + quality on revised plan; architecture and systems can be skipped unless scope expands. 16b must merge before 16c executes (see cross-plan section in 16c review).
